pi_items.source: Task description specifies this as the flow to create
Task description specifies this as the process to create

Web search confirms 18.5 MJ/kg is the standard net calorific value for oven-dry wood/biomass pellets. References: (1) Wood pellets industry sources report 18.4 MJ/kg (very close match); (2) Published biomass research shows 18.5-19 MJ/kg is standard for dry wood; (3) Published analyses report values ranging 17.03-18.54 MJ/kg with average 17.79 MJ/kg. The claimed value of 18.5 MJ/kg is accurate, realistic, and consistent with real-world biomass energy content standards.
Task description explicitly specifies: 'specify the amount as 1000.0' and 'change the unit to MJ'
Mathematically verified: 1000.0 MJ / 18.5 MJ/kg = 54.054054... kg. Python calculation confirms exact match to 3 decimal places.
Task description specifies 'export the inventory to a CSV file at ~/LCA_Results/biomass_inventory.csv' and export_result.sh verifies this path exists at /home/ga/LCA_Results/biomass_inventory.csv

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic workflow task where users create new OpenLCA entities from scratch. All metadata values have been verified: (1) The conversion factor of 18.5 MJ/kg for biomass fuel pellets is accurate and matches published standards for dry wood energy content; (2) The expected mass calculation of 54.054 kg is mathematically correct (1000 MJ ÷ 18.5 MJ/kg); (3) The flow and process names, input amount (1000 MJ), and output file path are all confirmed from the task instructions. No external real-world dataset is used—the task instructs users to create these entities and perform unit conversion calculations.
